name: Cranberry Wine Mold
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the cranberries in a strainer and remove any stems.
2. Place the berries in a large saucepan.
3. Add the sugar, red wine, and water to the saucepan and stir to blend.
4. Bring to boiling over medium heat.
5. Lower the heat and cook for 5 minutes, or until the berries burst. Remove from heat.
6. Stir in the raspberry-flavored gelatin until it dissolves.
7. Place the saucepan in the freezer and chill, stirring several times, for 30 minutes, or until the gelatin becomes syrupy.
8. Fold the chopped nuts into the gelatin mixture until well-blended.
9. Pour into an 8-cup mold or deep bowl. Refrigerate for four hours or overnight.
10. To unmold, run a thin-blade knife around the edge of the mold. Dip the mold into a pan of hot water for 30 seconds. Invert the mold onto the serving plate. Return the gelatin to the refrigerator until serving time.
